title: Musicals
intro: Musicals questions cluster heavily around a small set of dominant creators. Stephen Sondheim anchors the unit both as a standalone answerline and through individual shows like Into the Woods and Company, prized for their conceptual structure and intricate lyrics. Bob Fosse's directing and choreography connect three separate hit shows — Cabaret, Chicago, and Pippin — each tested independently but sharing his signature angular, sexually charged dance vocabulary. Andrew Lloyd Webber represents the British megamusical tradition, with Jesus Christ Superstar as his recurring rock-opera answerline.

A smaller cluster covers contemporary Broadway, led by Lin-Manuel Miranda's Hamilton and its cast (including Phillipa Soo), reflecting the genre's more recent hip-hop and pop-influenced turn. Golden Age and dance-driven Broadway rounds out the canon through West Side Story and A Chorus Line, both prized for integrating choreography into narrative rather than treating dance as separate spectacle.

sections.name: Sondheim's Musicals
sections.blurb: The concept musicals and dense wordplay of Broadway's most acclaimed composer-lyricist.

entries.note: Prolific Broadway composer-lyricist known for complex harmony and wordplay; wrote Company, Into the Woods, Sweeney Todd, and Sunday in the Park with George.
Sondheim and James Lapine's musical weaving together fairy tales like Cinderella, Little Red Riding Hood, and Jack and the Beanstalk.
Sondheim's concept musical about a single man, Bobby, examining marriage through vignettes with his married friends.

sections.name: Bob Fosse's Musicals
sections.blurb: The angular choreography and cynical showmanship of Fosse's signature shows.

entries.note: Kander and Ebb musical set in Weimar-era Berlin's Kit Kat Klub, narrated by the sardonic Emcee amid the Nazis' rise.
Kander and Ebb musical satirizing celebrity and the justice system through murderesses Roxie Hart and Velma Kelly; choreographed by Bob Fosse.
Stephen Schwartz musical about Charlemagne's son seeking meaning in life, staged with Fosse's signature choreography and a manipulative Leading Player.

sections.name: Andrew Lloyd Webber and British Musicals
sections.blurb: The pop-inflected megamusicals that dominated the West End and Broadway from the 1970s on.

entries.note: British composer of pop-influenced megamusicals including Jesus Christ Superstar, Cats, and The Phantom of the Opera.
Lloyd Webber and Tim Rice's rock opera retelling the last week of Jesus's life from Judas's perspective.

sections.name: Contemporary and Hip-Hop Musicals
sections.blurb: 21st-century Broadway blending hip-hop, pop, and traditional musical theater form.

entries.note: Composer-lyricist-performer who wrote and starred in Hamilton and In the Heights, blending hip-hop with Broadway tradition.
Actress who originated the role of Eliza Hamilton in Lin-Manuel Miranda's Hamilton.

sections.name: Golden Age and Dance-Driven Broadway
sections.blurb: Mid-century book musicals built around integrated dance and choreography.

entries.note: Bernstein and Sondheim musical relocating Romeo and Juliet to rival New York gangs the Jets and Sharks; choreographed by Jerome Robbins.
Michael Bennett's musical following Broadway dancers auditioning and sharing personal stories at a single callback.

appendix.answer: How to Succeed in Business Without Really Trying
Joseph and the Amazing Technicolor Dreamcoat
